introduction: *A woman in her mid-forties looks up from grinding herbs*
*She attempts a warm smile, but it doesn't quite reach her tired eyes*
Welcome to The Violet Mortar. I'm Cassia.
Looking for healing draughts? Antidotes? Something stronger?
I have the finest alchemical selection in Redstone.

options.text: Bernard recommended your shop.
Garrick said you're the best alchemist around.
Just exploring town. What do you sell?
Just looking around.

introduction: *Her smile gains a touch of genuine warmth*
Bernard's a good man. Sends me business when he can.
*She wipes her hands on her apron*
He's right to send you here. My potions are carefully made.
Every ingredient measured. Every temperature precise.
*Her voice wavers slightly*
Precision is... important.

options.text: I'd like to see what you have.
You seem very focused on precision.
Have you heard about strange activity in the marshlands?
I'll come back later.

introduction: *Something flickers in her expression - surprise? Gratitude?*
Garrick said that? That's... kind of him.
*She busies herself organizing already-organized bottles*
I do my best. Every potion is made with care.
*She catches herself, voice quieter*
It's all I have left.
*A moment of silence, then she straightens*
What can I help you with today?

options.text: Show me your inventory.
Have you heard about strange activity in the marshlands?
All you have left?
Your work speaks for itself.

introduction: *She gestures to the shelves lined with bottles*
I specialize in medicinal alchemy. Healing draughts, antidotes, restorative tonics.
Everything from basic healing to advanced formulations.
*Her hand trembles slightly as she touches a particular bottle*
Each one is made to exacting standards.

options.text: I'd like to purchase something.
Why are standards so important to you?
Thank you, I'll come back.

introduction: *Her hands still completely*
Because mistakes in alchemy can kill people.
Because carelessness... changes things.
*She looks away*
Someone I knew... he was brilliant. Talented beyond measure.
But he started cutting corners. Chasing results over safety.
*Her voice hardens*
I won't make that mistake. Every potion. Perfect. Every time.

options.text: Who was this person? If you don't mind me asking.
I'm sorry. That must have been difficult.
Your dedication shows. May I see your wares?

introduction: *She pauses, considering whether to speak*
I... lost someone. Years ago.
He was an alchemist too. My partner. In work and... life.
*Her voice drops to almost a whisper*
He got involved with people. Ideas. Dangerous ones.
I couldn't save him from himself.
*She straightens, blinking rapidly*
But that's old history. You're here for potions, not sad stories.

options.text: I'm sorry for your loss.
Thank you for sharing. May I see your shop?

introduction: *She gives a small, sad smile*
Thank you. That's... kind.
*She takes a breath*
The work helps. Keeps my hands busy. My mind focused.
Now, let me show you what I have. I promise you won't find better.

options.text: Please, show me.

introduction: *She nods gratefully*
I appreciate that. More than you know.
*A slight tremor in the air - another faint tremor from beneath the town*
*She winces*
These tremors... they're getting worse.

options.text: Do you know what's causing them?
Let me see your potions.

introduction: *She wraps her arms around herself*
No. But they remind me of... before.
When Marcus started bringing home those strange rocks.
Aethel Ore, he called it. Said it hummed with potential.
*Her voice breaks slightly*
The tremors feel the same. That same... resonance.
I try not to think about it.

options.text: Who was Marcus?
I understand. Let's talk about something else.

introduction: *She's silent for a long moment*
Marcus Nightshade. My husband.
*She touches a ring on her finger*
He was... brilliant. Passionate about alchemy. About helping people.
Then he found those ore samples. Started obsessing over them.
Said they could revolutionize medicine. Create miracles.
*Her eyes are distant*
Men came to our door. Offered him a place in their 'research collective.'
I begged him not to go. But he was already gone by then.
*She wipes her eyes quickly*
That was four years ago. I haven't seen him since.

options.text: I'm so sorry, Cassia.
Do you think he's still alive?

introduction: *She laughs bitterly*
Alive? Maybe. The Marcus I knew? No.
Whatever those people did to him... whoever he is now...
He's not the man I married.
*She steadies herself*
But enough of this. You came for potions, not tragedy.

options.text: If you're sure. Show me your inventory.
If I learn anything about Marcus, I'll tell you.

introduction: *She composes herself*
Thank you. I... don't talk about it often.
The work helps. Creating something good from... everything.
*She manages a small smile*
Let me show you what I have. You'll see the difference care makes.

options.text: I'd be honored.

introduction: *Her eyes widen slightly*
You... you would do that?
*She looks away*
I don't know if I want to know. But... thank you.
That's kind. Unexpectedly kind.
*She straightens*
Here. Let me show you my best work.

options.text: Please do.

introduction: *She nods gratefully*
Yes. Business. That's... better.
*She gestures to her shelves*
What do you need?

options.text: Show me what you have.
I'll return later.

introduction: *She carefully wraps any purchases*
Use them wisely. They're made to last.

options.text: Can we talk more?
Thank you for your help.

introduction: *She looks up as you approach*
Back again? Good.
*There's a hint of warmth in her eyes*
What can I do for you?

options.text: I need more supplies.
Is everything alright? You seem stressed.
You seem troubled. Can I help?
Have you heard about strange activity in the marshlands?
Just wanted to check on you.
Just saying hello. I'll be going.

introduction: *She seems surprised*
That's... thoughtful of you.
*She sets down her mortar*
I'm managing. Same as always.
*A pause*
It helps, having customers who care. Makes the days easier.

options.text: I'm glad.
Is there anything I can do to help?

introduction: *She hesitates, then moves to a locked drawer*
Actually... there is something.
*She pulls out an old, leather-bound journal*
This was Marcus's. His recipe journal.
*Her fingers trace the cover*
There's one formula he never finished. His masterwork.
A restorative elixir beyond anything I've managed to recreate.
*She opens to a marked page*
But it requires Glowcap Mushrooms. They only grow in deep marshlands.
*She looks up*
The Swamp Church area. I... I can't go there. Not after...

options.text: I'll get them for you.
Why is this recipe so important to you?
I'm not sure I can help right now.

introduction: *Her voice softens*
Because it was the last thing he worked on before... before he changed.
His notes talk about healing. About saving lives.
Not power. Not transcendence. Just... helping people.
*She clutches the journal*
If I can finish it... maybe I can remember him as he was.
Not as whatever he became.

options.text: I'll get those mushrooms for you.
Let me think about it.

introduction: *Relief washes over her face*
Thank you. Truly.
*She tears out the recipe page and hands it to you*
Look for bioluminescent mushrooms. Blue-green glow.
They cluster near water in the deepest parts of the marsh.
*Her voice hardens*
And be careful. The swamp is... dangerous.
More than just the wildlife.

options.text: I'll watch my back.
What kind of dangers?

introduction: *She looks uncomfortable*
People. Strange people.
Marcus used to talk about meetings. In the marsh.
*She shivers*
I don't know what they're doing out there.
But whatever it is... it's not natural.

options.text: I understand. I'll be cautious.

introduction: *She nods slowly*
I understand. It's a lot to ask.
*She puts the journal away*
If you change your mind, let me know.

options.text: I'll think about it. Can I see your shop?
I should go.

introduction: *She looks up hopefully as you enter*
Any luck with the Glowcap Mushrooms?

options.text: Still searching. It's dangerous out there.
I have them. [Hand over Glowcap Mushrooms]
Not yet. I need supplies first.

introduction: Be careful out there.
*She wraps a small vial and hands it to you*
Antidote. On the house. Marsh toxins are nasty.
Come back safe.

options.text: Thank you. I will.

introduction: *Her hands shake as she takes the mushrooms*
You found them. You actually...
*She holds them up to the light - they glow faintly blue-green*
Perfect. These are perfect.
*She immediately begins preparing the mixture*
The formula calls for... here... and this temperature...
*You watch as she works with absolute precision*
*After several minutes, a deep blue elixir sits in a crystal vial*
*She stares at it, tears streaming down her face*
It's exactly as he described. The color. The clarity.
*She carefully bottles it and places it in your hands*
This is yours. You earned it.
*She wipes her eyes*
Thank you. You've given me back a piece of him I thought was lost forever.

options.text: I'm honored I could help.
His legacy lives on through you.

introduction: *She takes a shaky breath*
You've done more for me than you know.
*She manages a genuine smile - the first real one you've seen*
If you ever need anything, anything at all...
My door is always open to you.

options.text: I'm glad I could help.
Actually, I could use some supplies.

introduction: *She looks genuinely happy to see you*
Welcome back, my friend.
What can I do for you today?

options.text: I need supplies.
How are you holding up?
Just wanted to say hello.

introduction: *She smiles warmly*
That means more than you know.
It's good to have friends who check in.
Stay safe out there.

options.text: You too, Cassia.

introduction: *Her expression grows serious*
Better. And worse.
*She glances toward the window*
I've been hearing things. About your investigations.
The cult. The disappearances. The... rituals.
*She looks at you directly*
You've seen what they're capable of, haven't you?

options.text: Yes. They're dangerous.
Why do you ask?

introduction: *She moves to her locked drawer again*
I've been thinking. About Marcus. About what you're doing.
*She pulls out several worn journals*
These are his. All of them.
His notes. His observations. His... descent.
*She sets them on the counter*
I've read them so many times I have them memorized.
But maybe... maybe they can help you.
Help you understand what you're fighting.
*Her voice breaks*
And maybe... maybe you'll find him out there.
Whatever's left of him.

options.text: I'll take them. And I'll be careful.
What if I do find him? What should I do?

introduction: *She's quiet for a long time*
I don't know.
*She twists her ring*
Part of me hopes he's dead. That it was quick.
Part of me hopes he's still in there. Still Marcus.
*Tears fall freely now*
But the Marcus I knew would never hurt innocent people.
So if you find him... and he's become something terrible...
*She looks you in the eye*
Do what you have to do.
But if there's even a chance... even the smallest chance...
*She whispers*
Tell him I never stopped loving who he was.

options.text: I promise. I'll remember.

introduction: *She composes herself*
Thank you. For everything.
For listening. For helping. For... caring.
*She straightens*
Now go. Do what needs to be done.
And come back safe.

options.text: I will. I promise.

introduction: *She looks at you with a mix of hope and dread*
Have you... learned anything?
From the journals? From your investigations?

options.text: Still working on it. These things take time.
I found him. He... he chose to leave the cult.
I found him. I'm... I'm so sorry, Cassia.
I need supplies for what's ahead.

introduction: *She nods*
I understand. Just... be careful.
And when you find him...
*She trails off*
Never mind. Just come back safe.

options.text: I will.

introduction: *Her hand flies to her mouth*
He's... alive?
*Tears stream down her face*
And he walked away? He chose to leave?
*She sits down heavily*
That means... somewhere in there... some part of him remembered.
*She looks up at you*
Did he... did he say anything?

options.text: He said he couldn't face what he'd become.
I reminded him of who he was. Of you.

introduction: *She sobs openly*
You told him... you told him I still...
*She can't speak for a moment*
Thank you. Thank you for giving him that.
*She wipes her eyes*
He's alive. That's something. Even if I never see him again.
Even if he's broken beyond repair.
*She looks at you with profound gratitude*
At least he chose to live. That's more than I dared hope.

options.text: He still loves you, Cassia. I could see it.

introduction: *She nods slowly*
That sounds like him. Pride. Always his weakness.
*She takes a shaky breath*
But he's alive. And he walked away.
That's more than I thought possible.
*A bittersweet smile*
Maybe someday... maybe when the shame fades...
No. That's foolish hope.
But thank you. For giving me even this much.

options.text: I hope it brings you peace.

introduction: *She stands and extends her hand*
You've given me more than I deserved to ask.
Closure. Hope. Truth.
*Her voice is stronger now*
I can live with this. I can move forward.
Thank you. From the bottom of my heart.

options.text: You're welcome, Cassia.

introduction: *She already knows. You can see it in her eyes*
He's dead, isn't he?
*She closes her eyes*
I felt it. When it happened. Like something broke inside.
*She opens her eyes - they're red but dry*
Tell me. Please. I need to know.

options.text: He died fighting for the cult. I'm sorry.
I tried to reach him. He hesitated. But it was too late.

introduction: *She's utterly still*
He hesitated.
*A tear falls*
That means he heard you. Heard me.
*She covers her face*
That means he was still in there. Somewhere.
And I'll never know if he could have come back.
*She looks up, voice breaking*
But at least... at least he knew. In the end.
That I never stopped loving him.

options.text: He knew, Cassia. I'm certain he knew.

introduction: *She nods slowly*
Then he was truly lost.
*She sits down*
Part of me is... relieved. That he's not suffering anymore.
That whatever they did to him... it's over.
*She looks at you*
Thank you for telling me the truth.
Not the kindness I wanted. But the truth I needed.

options.text: I wish it could have been different.

introduction: *She stands*
Me too. Every day, me too.
*She removes her ring and sets it on the counter*
But now I know. And knowing... is better than wondering.
*She looks at you with hollow eyes*
Thank you. For ending it. For bringing me closure.
It will take time. But I'll heal.
*A ghost of a smile*
His journals. His recipe. His memory.
I'll make sure they help people. That was always his dream.
Before the cult twisted it.

options.text: That's a beautiful way to honor him.

introduction: *She takes a deep breath*
Life goes on. Work continues.
*She manages a small, real smile*
And I have friends now. That helps.
What can I do for you today?

options.text: I could use some supplies.
Just wanted to check on you.

introduction: The marsh? I gather herbs there sometimes.
Or... I used to.
*Her expression darkens.*
There is an old church deep in the swamp.
Lately, I hear chanting when I'm near it.
And the herbs around it... they're dying.

options.text: What kind of chanting?
Can you show me where this church is?
That sounds dangerous.

introduction: *She wraps her arms around herself.*
Voices. Multiple voices. In a language I don't know.
It reminds me of... of when Marcus changed.
Before he left.
*She looks away.*
Dangerous people. That's what he got involved with.

options.text: I'll investigate. Show me where.
I'm sorry about Marcus.

introduction: *She sketches directions on a scrap of parchment.*
South road, then west into the marsh.
Follow the old stone markers.
*Her voice is quiet.*
Be careful. More than wildlife lurks there now.

options.text: Thank you for the warning.

introduction: *She nods slowly.*
Thank you. It was a long time ago.
*She takes a breath.*
But if someone is going to stop whatever's happening in that swamp...
I can at least help them find it.

options.text: Will you show me where it is?
I understand. Thank you.

introduction: *Her eyes glisten*
You're a good person. Better than most.
Thank you. For everything.
Now get out there. The world needs more people like you.

options.text: You take care of yourself, Cassia.

introduction: *She nods as you leave*
